title: Code Points, Grapheme Clusters, Character Categories, and Labels · Code Recall
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs code points, grapheme clusters, character categories, and labels from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for honestly labeled text-metrics panel after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Distinguish bytes, code points, extended grapheme clusters, display cells, letters, and stakeholder-defined characters.
Use Unicode categories or explicit predicates to separate letters, marks, numbers, punctuation, symbols, separators, and controls.
State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof. This does not satisfy Metric Label Evidence for honestly labeled text-metrics panel.
The record names the assumption, applies Metric Label Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Metric Label Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Metric Label Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Bookbot team handles case 5601 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to label and reconcile code-point, grapheme, and letter metrics without overstating browser segmentation; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Label every metric with unit, inclusion policy, input version, and reconciliation or non-reconciliation rule.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Metric Label Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Metric Label Evidence through the concrete honestly labeled text-metrics panel case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Metric Label Evidence, keep Metric Label Evidence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Label every metric with unit, inclusion policy, input version, and reconciliation or non-reconciliation rule.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Metric Label Evidence, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Metric Label Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Metric Label Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Bookbot team handles case 5601 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to label and reconcile code-point, grapheme, and letter metrics without overstating browser segmentation; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Label every metric with unit, inclusion policy, input version, and reconciliation or non-reconciliation rule.

For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Metric Label Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A report can call every text unit characters as long as the number is correct.
